acceptance: A process/runtime-owned libcurl multi handle is initialized, driven, and cleaned up safely.
verification: `cmake --preset default` passed. `cmake --build --preset default` passed. CurlSession owns the multi handle, registers libcurl socket and timer callbacks, and cleans up socket registrations before cleanup.
notes: The socket callbacks bridge through the current EventPoll layer until CM-004 old poller cleanup is possible.

acceptance: A single ordinary URL transfer can be represented as a curl easy handle with callbacks mapped to aria2-next state.
verification: Focused test `testInitiateConnectionFactoryUsesCurlForHttp` passed and verifies HTTP routing creates CurlDownloadCommand.
notes: HTTP/HTTPS are routed first. FTP/SFTP remain deferred to CM-007.

scope: Data callback and piece write boundary
acceptance: Received bytes write through existing storage safely, with offset and length truth preserved.

acceptance: curl errors, HTTP status failures, retryable failures, and completion events map to existing result semantics.
verification: CurlDownloadCommand maps libcurl failures and HTTP status >=400 through DownloadFailureException and marks completion through RequestGroup/SegmentMan state. Build and focused tests passed.
notes: Retry taxonomy will be refined during CM-006 HTTP feature parity.
